structured.description: A `Texture` class that loads and manages texture data for use in an OpenGL application. It uses the `GL11` and `GL30` packages to bind and manipulate textures. The `loadTexture()` method reads image data from a file using `ImageIO`, converts it to a `ByteBuffer`, and then uses `glTexImage2D()` to upload the data as a texture to the GPU. The `bind()` and `bind(int samplerSlot)` methods bind the texture to a specific slot in the GPU's texture array.

items.description: provides a simple way to handle textures in a OpenGL environment. It allows for loading textures from files and binding them to a texture slot in the GPU. The class also provides methods for getting the ID of the loaded texture.
items.name: Texture

items.description: does not have any specific functionality as it is already implemented by the Java Language. Therefore, it does not perform any operations or actions.

usage.description: In this example, the method `finalize` will be called when the instance of the `MyClass` is garbage collected. The method will print a message to the console indicating that the instance has been finalized and also release any resource allocated by the class.

items.name: finalize

items.description: 0 is called, no other information is provided.

usage.description: In this example, we are calling the bind method and passing in 0 as a parameter to indicate that we want to use texture unit 0. We then call the bindTexture method on our texture object and pass in the id of our texture. This will bind our texture to texture unit 0 so that it can be used when rendering with OpenGL.

items.name: bind

items.description: sets the active texture slot to a specific index (0-31) and binds the specified texture ID to that slot using the `glBindTexture()` method.

params.name: samplerSlot
params.description: 0-based index of a texture slot in the GPU, with valid values ranging from 0 to 31.

items.name: bind

items.description: returns the value of the `id` field.

returns.description: an integer representing the value of the `id` field.

items.name: getID

items.description: loads a texture from an image file, allocates memory for it, and binds it to a GPU texture object. It also sets up texture wrapping and filtering parameters.

params.name: fileName
params.description: filename of the image to be loaded and processed by the `loadTexture()` method.

returns.description: an integer ID representing a loaded texture.

items.name: loadTexture

structured.description: A camera class with abstract methods for calculating view and projection matrices, as well as a method for adjusting the camera to the viewport. The class also contains a `Transform` class for storing camera transformations.

items.description: is an abstract class that represents a camera in a 3D space. It has several fields and methods for calculating and manipulating matrices related to the camera's position, rotation, and projection. The class also has an abstract method for calculating the camera's projection matrix and another method for adjusting the camera to fit within a specified viewport.
items.name: Camera

items.description: computes and returns a `Matrix4f` object representing the view projection transformation, which combines the camera's view matrix and projection matrix. If the `viewProjectionMat4` is null or the transform has changed, it recalculates the view matrix.

returns.description:
a `Matrix4f` object representing the view projection matrix.

* `viewProjectionMat4`: This is a matrix object representing the view projection transformation. It contains the view and projection matrices combined into a single 4x4 matrix.
* `transform`: This is an instance of the `Transform` class, which stores the transformation information used to calculate the view matrix. The `hasChanged()` method is used to check if the transformation has changed since the last calculation.

In summary, the `getViewProjection` function returns a 4x4 matrix object representing the view projection transformation, which is calculated based on the current transformation state.

usage.code:
Camera camera = new PerspectiveCamera();
Matrix4f viewProjectionMat4 = camera.getViewProjection();

items.name: getViewProjection

items.description: calculates a view matrix that combines a camera's rotation and translation with the projection matrix to produce the final view matrix for rendering.

returns.description:
a matrix that represents the view transformation of a camera, combining rotation and translation components.

* The output is a 4x4 matrix, representing the view matrix.
* The first three columns represent the rotation of the camera relative to the world coordinate system, while the fourth column represents the translation of the camera relative to the origin of the world coordinate system.
* The matrix is generated by multiplying the projection matrix with the rotation matrix and then with the translation matrix.

In summary, the `calculateViewMatrix` function returns a view matrix that takes into account both the rotation and translation of the camera, allowing for accurate rendering of 3D scenes from different viewpoints.

usage.code:
Camera cam = new Camera();
cam.getTranslationMatrix() // This will return a matrix that contains the position of the camera in 3D space
cam.calculateProjectionMatrix(data) // This will return a projection matrix for the given data struct
cam.calculateViewMatrix() //This will return a view matrix based on the projection matrix and the translation matrix obtained from getTranslationMatrix().

items.name: calculateViewMatrix

items.description: generates a 4x4 transformation matrix representing the negative translation of the camera position.

returns.description:
a 4x4 transformation matrix representing the translation of the camera position.

The function returns a 4x4 matrix representing a translation in 3D space. The upper-left 3x3 submatrix represents the translation vector in world coordinates.

The lower-right 1x3 submatrix contains the components of the translation vector along the X, Y, and Z axes, respectively. These values represent the offset from the origin in each dimension.

In summary, the `getTranslationMatrix` function generates a matrix that represents a translation in 3D space, with the upper-left submatrix containing the translation vector in world coordinates and the lower-right submatrix containing the components of the translation vector along the X, Y, and Z axes.

items.name: getTranslationMatrix

items.description: returns the `transform` object, which represents a transformation of some kind.

returns.description:
a `Transform` object representing the transformation of the input data.

The `transform` variable is an object of the `Transform` class, which represents a transformation matrix in 3D space. It has several attributes, including the translation vector (x, y, z), rotation angle around each axis (x, y, z), and scaling factors for x, y, and z. These attributes can be used to perform various transformations on 3D objects, such as translating, rotating, or scaling them.

items.name: getTransform

items.description: is an abstract class that serves as a base for other camera classes in the file. It provides an abstract method called `getAsMatrix4()` which returns a Matrix4f object, but does not define any fields or methods of its own.
items.name: CameraStruct

structured.description: A class `Camera3D` that extends the `Camera` class and provides additional functionality for a 3D camera. The code includes methods for calculating the camera's projection matrix, adjusting the viewport, processing input events such as mouse clicks and keyboard keys, and moving the camera. The code also includes a inner class `CameraStruct3D` that represents the camera's state, including its position, orientation, and field of view.

items.description: is an extension of the Camera Class that includes additional functionality for handling 3D camera inputs and calculations. It has a constructor that takes fov, aspect, zNear, and zFar parameters to initialize the camera's projection matrix. The class also has a method adjustToViewport that adjusts the camera's projection matrix based on the viewport dimensions. Additionally, the class has a protected inner class CameraStruct3D that contains the camera's projection matrix, which is used in the calculateProjectionMatrix method.
items.name: Camera3D

items.description: calculates a projection matrix based on input data from a `CameraStruct`.

params.name: data
params.description:
3D camera information that is used to calculate the projection matrix.

* `getAsMatrix4()` is an instance method that returns a `Matrix4f` object representing the 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix for the camera.

returns.description:
a Matrix4f object representing the projection matrix based on the provided camera data.

The `Matrix4f` object that is returned represents the projection matrix in 3D space. It contains the necessary values for transforming 3D points and vectors into screen coordinates. The matrix has four rows and four columns, with each element representing a value in the range of -1 to 1.

items.name: calculateProjectionMatrix

items.description: adjusts the camera's projection and view matrices to fit within the specified width and height of the viewport, while maintaining the aspect ratio of the scene.

params.name: width
params.description: 2D viewport width.

params.name: height
params.description: vertical dimension of the viewport for which the camera's projection and view matrices are calculated.

usage.description: This code uses the method adjustToViewport from Camera3D and is passed an int for width and a different int for height. The code then sets the aspect ratio of the camera to the viewport aspect ratio, calculates the projection matrix with the values set by this.values, then calculates the view matrix. Finally, it applies the glViewport method to the width, height, and 0, 0 parameters of the GL11 class.

items.name: adjustToViewport

items.description: is an extension of the CameraStruct class and provides additional functionality for handling 3D camera movements. It has fields for fov, aspect, zNear, and zFar, which are used to calculate the camera's perspective matrix. The class also provides a method for getting the camera's perspective matrix as a Matrix4f object.
items.name: CameraStruct3D

items.description: initializes a `Matrix4f` object with a perspective projection matrix based on field of view (`fov`), aspect ratio, near and far z-coordinates.

returns.description:
a 4x4 matrix representing a perspective projection.

* The `Matrix4f` object returned represents a 4x4 matrix with elements representing homogeneous coordinates.
* The matrix is initialized using the `initPerspective` method, which sets the field of view (fov), aspect ratio (aspect), near clip plane (zNear), and far clip plane (zFar) values.
* The resulting matrix is a perspective projection matrix, used to transform 3D points into screen coordinates for rendering purposes.

usage.description: In this example, the `getAsMatrix4()` method is called on an instance of `Camera3D` with the given field of view (fov), aspect ratio, and near/far clipping planes. The resulting `projectionMatrix` can then be used for rendering 3D graphics using OpenGL.

items.name: getAsMatrix4

items.description: processes input from the keyboard and mouse, applying rotations to an object's transform based on user input, and moving the object along a direction based on the keyboard inputs.

params.name: dt
params.description: time step of the simulation, which is used to calculate the movement of the object based on its velocity.

params.name: speed
params.description: 3D movement speed of the object being controlled by the player, and it is multiplied by the time interval `dt` to determine the total distance traveled during that time.

params.name: sens
params.description: sensitivity of the movement, which determines how much the object will move based on the user's input.

items.name: processInput

items.description: updates the position of an object by adding a directional vector multiplied by a scalar amount to its current position.

params.name: dir
params.description:
3D direction in which the object should be moved, with the magnitude of the movement specified by the `amt` parameter.

* `dir`: A `Vector3f` object representing the direction of movement. It has three components: x, y, and z, which correspond to the horizontal, vertical, and forward motion, respectively.
* `amt`: An integer value representing the amount of movement to be applied in the specified direction.

params.name: amt
params.description: amount of movement to be applied to the object's position along the specified direction.

usage.description: In this example, we first create a Vector3f object representing the forward direction (0, 1, 0). We then set the amount to move in that direction to 5.0f. Finally, we call the move method with these inputs, which will move the camera in the forward direction by 5 units.

items.name: move

structured.description: A 3D graphics program using the LWJGL library. It creates a display, initializes GL, sets up a camera and model, loads a texture, and renders a scene. The scene is updated and rendered in a loop until the user closes the window.

items.description: of this project is responsible for creating and rendering a 3D scene using Java and the LWJGL library. It sets up the scene, loads a shader, and creates a world and a camera. The loop function updates the position of the camera and renders the scene using the shader. The program also includes an exit function to shut down the application when the user presses the escape key.
items.name: Main

items.description: initializes display and graphics libraries, enters an infinite loop for rendering, and then exits with a successful status code of 0.

params.name: args
params.description:
0 or more command-line arguments passed to the program by the user, which are then ignored and have no effect on the function's execution.

* Length: The length of the `args` array is determined at compile-time and is always non-zero.
* Elements: Each element in `args` is a string representing an command-line argument passed to the program.
* Types: Each element in `args` is a `String`, indicating that the corresponding argument must be a string value.

items.name: main

items.description: initializes a display with a resolution of 1920x1080, sets vsync enabled, and prints the version of GL supported by the system using `GL11.glGetString()`.
items.name: initDisplay

items.description: sets up the GL context for a 3D rendering application, including setting the clear color, enabling culling and depth testing, loading a shader, and creating a texture and vertices for a 3D model.
items.name: initGL

items.description: continuously runs a loop while a close request is not received and the escape key is not pressed. It updates and renders the display, displays the frame rate, and clears the color and depth buffers.
items.name: loop

items.description: updates the position of an object (`w`) based on input processing and transformation.

params.name: dt
params.description: delta time, which is a measure of the elapsed time since the last frame and is used to update the object's position and transform.

items.name: update

items.description: renders a 3D model using the OpenGL API. It sets up the rendering context, binds the model's vertex buffer, and draws the model using the model matrix.
items.name: render

items.description: terminates the application with the specified exit code, which is passed as an argument.

params.name: status
params.description: value to be passed as an argument to the `System.exit()` method, which terminates the Java application with the specified exit status.

items.name: exit

structured.description: A `Model` class that represents 3D models in an Opengl environment. The class has several methods for loading and drawing models, as well as getting information about the model's vertex array object (VAO) and size. The code also includes utility methods for creating and manipulating buffers used for storing vertex and index data.

items.description: is used to manage vertex and element arrays for rendering 3D objects. It provides methods for binding and unbinding vertex array objects (VAOs) and elements array buffer objects (EABOs), as well as storing data in these buffers. Additionally, the class provides a way to load data from an array of vertices and indices and create a new Model instance.
items.name: Model

items.description: binds a vertex array object, enables two vertex attributes, and calls either `glDrawArrays` or `glDrawElements` to render a triangle-based shape.
items.name: draw

items.description: enables vertex attributes 0 and 1 for rendering.
items.name: enableAttribs

items.description: disables both vertex attribute arrays for a GL20 context.
items.name: disableAttribs

items.description: returns the value of a variable `vao`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the `vao` field.

items.name: getVAO

items.description: returns the value of the `size` field, which represents the number of elements in a collection or array.

returns.description: the value of the `size` field.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
int size = model.getSize();

items.name: getSize

items.description: loads a model into memory from a set of vertex and index data. It creates a Vertex Array Object (VAO), stores the indices, and then stores the vertex data before unbinding the VAO. The function returns a `Model` object containing the loaded data.

params.name: vertices
params.description:
3D model's geometry as an array of float values, which are stored and bound to a Vertex Array Object (VAO) for rendering.

* `vertices`: An array of `float` values representing 3D vertices for a model.
* `indices`: An array of `int` values representing the indices of the vertices in the array.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the input array, which is also the length of the `indices` array.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D model's index data for the vertices, which is stored and bound to a Vertex Array Object (VAO) by the function for efficient rendering.

* `indices`: An array of integers representing the indices of the vertices in the model.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the model, which can be calculated by summing up the lengths of all the arrays that `indices` points to.

returns.description:
a `Model` object representing a 3D model.

* The output is a `Model` object, which represents a 3D model in the scene.
* The `vao` field of the `Model` object contains the handle to the Vertex Array Object (VAO) used to store and manipulate the vertices of the model.
* The `v_count` field of the `Model` object indicates the number of vertices in the model.

In summary, the output of the `load` function is a `Model` object with a handle to a VAO and the number of vertices in the model.

items.name: load

items.description: generates a new vertex array object (VBO) using the `glGenVertexArrays` method and binds it to the current context using `glBindVertexArray`. The returned value is the ID of the newly created VBO.

returns.description: an integer value representing a unique vertex array object (Vao) handle.

items.name: createVAO

items.description: 1) generates a vertex buffer object (VBO), 2) binds it, 3) stores array data in it, and 4) sets vertex attribute pointers for rendering.

params.name: attrib
params.description: attribute index of the vertex data to be stored in the VBO.

params.name: data
params.description:
2D array of floating-point values that will be stored in the VBO as an array of three floats, with each float value located at a specific index in the array.

* The input `data` is an array of 3D floats, indicating that it has three dimensions (x, y, and z) and contains floating-point values.
* The `createFlippedBuffer` method is used to create a buffer object from the input data, which is then passed as a parameter to the `GL15.glBufferData` function for storage in the VBO.
* The `GL20.glVertexAttribPointer` function is called twice, once for each attribute (x and y) of the input data, to set the corresponding vertex attributes for rendering. The second attribute (`z`) is not included as it is not needed for this specific use case.

items.name: storeData

items.description: stores an array of indices in a buffer for static draw operations using OpenGL's `GL_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER` binding.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D vertex positions of a mesh to be stored in an element array buffer for rendering in a 3D graphics pipeline.

* `GL15.glGenBuffers()` creates a new buffer object with the method `GL15.glGenBuffers()`.
* `GL15.glBindBuffer(GL15.GL_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER, ibo)` binds the newly created buffer object to the element array buffer slot using `GL15.glBindBuffer()`.
* `GL15.glBufferData(GL15.GL_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER, Util.createFlippedBuffer(indices), GL15.GL_STATIC_DRAW)` copies the deserialized input `indices` into a buffer object using `GL15.glBufferData()`, and then binds it to the element array buffer slot using `GL15.glBindBuffer()`. The `Util.createFlippedBuffer(indices)` method creates a flipped copy of the input `indices`.

items.name: storeIndices

items.description: disables the vertex array object (VAO) bound to the current program by passing a value of `0` to the `glBindVertexArray` method.
items.name: unbindVAO

structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages OpenGL shaders. It provides methods for binding the shader program, uniform floats and matrices, and validating the program. The code also includes utility methods for loading shader source code from files and creating shader programs.

items.description: provides functionality for creating and managing shaders in OpenGL. It allows for loading and validating shaders, as well as binding and unbinding them from the program. Additionally, it provides methods for uniform and matrix uniforms.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to a specific OpenGL context, allowing for efficient rendering and manipulation of graphics data.
items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `program`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the program.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: takes a string parameter `name` and an array of float values `vals`. It dynamically generates GL calls to set uniform values based on the length of the `vals` array.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location being set, which is used to identify the location in the OpenGL state where the values in the `vals` array are being assigned.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: uniformly sets a 4x4 floating-point matrix to a specified location in memory using the `glUniformMatrix4` function from OpenGL.

params.name: name
params.description: 3D matrix's uniform location in the shader program, as specified by the GL20 class method `glUniformMatrix4`.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix to be uniformed across the current GL context.

* `name`: A string parameter indicating the uniform name to be used for the matrix.
* `mat`: A `Matrix4f` object representing a 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix. The matrix elements can be accessed as `mat.getLinearData()` and are stored in a flipped buffer when passed to `glUniformMatrix4`.

usage.code:
Matrix4f mat = new Matrix4f(); //Create a new matrix object
mat.setIdentity(); //Set the matrix to identity
Shader shader = Shader.loadShader("shaders/example"); //Load the example shader
shader.bind(); //Bind the shader program
shader.uniformMat4("modelView", mat); //Pass the modelView matrix to the shader
usage.description: In this example, a new Matrix4f object is created and set to identity using the setIdentity() method. The Shader.loadShader() method is then used to load an example shader from the "shaders/example" file path. Finally, the bind() method is called on the shader object to bind it to the current GL context, and the uniformMat4() method is used to pass the modelView matrix to the shader.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform in a program specified by an integer identifier using the `GL20.glGetUniformLocation` method.

params.name: name
params.description: 1D array index of the uniform location to retrieve in the OpenGL ES 2.0 program.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform variable in the program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader program from a file and validates it, returning a new shader object.

params.name: filename
params.description: path to a shader file that contains the vertex and fragment shaders for loading into the program.

returns.description:
a newly created shader object representing a compiled shader program.

* The function returns a `Shader` object that represents a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.
* The `Shader` object is an instance of the `com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.glutils.Shader` class, which provides a set of methods for manipulating and validating the shader program.
* The `program` field of the `Shader` object contains the handle to the created shader program.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a shader program and attaches it to a program handle. It compiles the shader source code and prints an error message if compilation fails.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader being created, with possible values of 0 for vertex shaders and 1 for fragment shaders.

params.name: src
params.description: 1:1 representation of the shader source code that is to be compiled and executed by the `glShaderSource()` function.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program that the loaded shader will be attached to and used by.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program object and checks its linking and validation status.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D rendering program to be validated and linked with the OpenGL API.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads a file and returns its contents as a string.

params.name: file
params.description: file from which the text is to be read.

returns.description: a string representation of the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText

structured.description:
a simplex-based implementation of the marching cubes algorithm for generating 3D meshes from a set of scattered points in a 2D space. It uses the G4 constant to control the size of the simplices and the number of corners in each simplex. The code calculates the contribution from each simplex corner to the final mesh, based on the coordinates of the scattered points and the gradient matrix at that point. These contributions are then summed up and scaled to cover the range [-1,1].

The code uses the `java.util` package for its hash functions and the `java.lang` package for mathematical operations. It also uses the `simplex` class to represent the simplices in a 3D space.

items.description: in C++ is a mathematical formula used to generate noise patterns. It takes in a set of parameters (x0, y0, z0, w0) and returns a value between -1 and 1 that represents the noise pattern at those coordinates. The formula works by calculating the hashed gradient indices of five simplex corners, then calculates the contribution from each corner using the dot product of the gradient vector and the simplex corner's coordinate. These contributions are then summed to generate the final noise value.
items.name: SimplexNoise

items.description: returns the nearest integer to a given double value, rounding down if positive and up if negative.

params.name: x
params.description: double value to be rounded, which is used to determine whether it should be rounded up or down to the nearest integer.

returns.description: an integer value between 0 and the input value, inclusive.

items.name: fastfloor

items.description: computes the dot product of a given vector (represented by `g`) and two other vectors (represented by `x` and `y`).

params.name: g
params.description: 2D coordinates of a point in the function.

params.name: x
params.description: 1st component of the input vector and is multiplied by the corresponding element of the `g` array to compute the output value.

params.name: y
params.description: 2nd coordinate of the point to perform dot product calculation with the given vector `g`.

returns.description: a double value calculated by multiplying the input `x` and `y` components of an integer array `g`.

items.name: dot

items.description: computes the dot product of a given vector (represented by the integer array `g`) and a scalar value `x`, `y`, or `z`. The result is a double value representing the dot product.

params.name: g
params.description: 3D coordinates of a point in the space, with each element of the array `g` corresponding to one dimension of the point (x, y, or z).

params.name: x
params.description: 0th element of the input array and multiplies its value with the input `x`.

params.name: y
params.description: 2nd component of the input vector and multiplies it with the result of the multiplication of the 1st component of the input vector and the `x` parameter, followed by the multiplication of the result with the `z` parameter.

params.name: z
params.description: 3rd dimension of the array `g`, and it is multiplied by `z` in the sum with `x` and `y`.

returns.description: a value representing the dot product of the given vectors.

items.name: dot

items.description: computes the dot product of four vectors, represented as integers `g[0-3]`, and scalars `x`, `y`, `z`, and `w`. It returns the result of the multiplication as a double value.

params.name: g
params.description: 4-dimensional vector that is being dot-producted with the given `x`, `y`, `z`, and `w` values.

params.name: x
params.description: 0-based index of the first element of an array of integers that is used to compute the dot product.

params.name: y
params.description: 2nd coordinate of the point being manipulated in the dot product calculation.

params.name: z
params.description: 3rd dimension of the input array `g`, and is multiplied by `w` before being added to the result of the function.

params.name: w
params.description: 4th coordinate of the dot product calculation.

returns.description: a scalar value representing the dot product of the given vectors.

items.name: dot

items.description: calculates a noise value based on the coordinates of two points in a 2D space, using a simplex noise algorithm. It returns a scaled value between -1 and 1.

params.name: xin
params.description: 2D coordinate of the point within the simplex cell where the noise is being evaluated, and it is used to calculate the hashed gradient indices for the three simplex corners.

params.name: yin
params.description: 2D coordinate of the point for which noise is being generated, and it is used in conjunction with the `xin` input parameter to determine the simplex cell that the point falls within.

returns.description: a scalar value in the range [-1, 1].

items.name: noise

items.description: computes a noise value for a given 3D point based on its coordinates and a Hashed Gradient Noise algorithm. It returns a scaled value between [-1,1].

params.name: xin
params.description: 3D space coordinates of a point where noise is being generated.

params.name: yin
params.description: 2D projection of the noise contribution from the `y` coordinate of the input point.

params.name: zin
params.description: 3D noise position, which is used to calculate the contribution from each of the four simplex corners to the final noise value.

returns.description: a scaled noise value between -1 and 1.

items.name: noise

items.description: calculates a noise gradient tensor at a given point in 3D space, based on the positions of five simplex corners and their hashed gradients.

params.name: x
params.description: 3D coordinates of a point in the simplex, which are used to compute the gradient contribution from each corner of the simplex.

params.name: y
params.description: 2D gradient of the function at the current point, which is used to calculate the contribution from each simplex corner in the Hashed Gradient Algorithm.

params.name: z
params.description: 3D coordinate of the current point in the mesh, which is used to compute the dot product with the gradient vector at each corner of the simplex and contribute to the final gradient descriptor value.

params.name: w
params.description: 4th coordinate of the point being evaluated, which is used to compute the dot product with the gradient vectors at each corner of the simplex and contribute to the final output value.

returns.description: a scalar value between -1 and 1, representing the noise magnitude at a given point in space.

items.name: noise

structured.description: A `Texture` class that loads an image from a file and creates a texture object in OpenGL. The texture is bound to a specific texture slot using the `bind()` method. The `getID()` method returns the ID of the created texture. The `loadTexture()` method reads an image from a file, converts it to an OpenGL-compatible format, and creates a texture object based on the converted data.

items.description: in the provided code is used to load and manage textures in an OpenGL environment. It allows for binding and unbinding of textures, as well as providing the ID of the loaded texture. The loadTexture method loads a texture from a specified file and returns the ID of the loaded texture.
items.name: Texture

items.description: 0 in the code is used to bind an object to a specified position in an array.

usage.description: In this example, the method bind is called on the "render" method to create a 2D texture and bind it to the OpenGL context.

items.name: bind

items.description: binds a texture to an OpenGL texture slot. The function first checks if the input `samplerSlot` is within the valid range (0-31) and then activates the corresponding texture slot using `glActiveTexture()` and binds the texture using `glBindTexture()`.

params.name: samplerSlot
params.description: 0-based index of a texture slot in the GPU, which the function binds to a specific texture id.

usage.code:
int samplerSlot = 1;
texture.bind(samplerSlot);
usage.description: This method takes a single parameter which is the slot number of the texture unit to bind the texture to. This method will then use the glActiveTexture function to set the active texture unit to the given slot and the glBindTexture function to bind the texture to that texture unit.

items.name: bind

items.description: returns the `id` field of an object.

returns.description: an integer representing the ID of the object.

usage.code:
Texture texture = new Texture("image.jpg");
int id = texture.getID();
usage.description: This would return the integer id of the texture, which can then be used to bind and manipulate it in OpenGL.

items.name: getID

items.description: loads an image file into a texture object using OpenGL. It reads the image data from a file, converts it to an OpenGL-compatible format, and binds it as a texture to the current rendering context.

params.name: fileName
params.description: file name of the image to load as a texture.

returns.description: an integer representing a valid texture ID.

items.name: loadTexture

structured.description: A `Timer` class with various methods for tracking frames per second (FPS) and time elapsed. The class uses the `Sys` package to get the current time in milliseconds and calculate FPS. The `init()` method initializes the timer, while the `update()` method calculates the delta time and updates the FPS counter. The `getDelta()` and `getFPS()` methods provide access to these values, and the `getTime()` method returns the total time elapsed.

items.description: provides a way to measure and update the frame rate of an application. It has several methods for calculating and updating the frame rate, including `init()`, `calculateDelta()`, `updateFPS()`, and `update()`. These methods allow for the measurement of time and the calculation of the delta between frames, as well as the updating of the frame rate counter.
items.name: Timer

items.description: calculates the seconds since the Unix epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC) based on the current system time and timer resolution.

returns.description: a long value representing the current time in milliseconds, adjusted for timer resolution.

items.name: getTimeS

items.description: initializes a variable `lastFPS` with the current value of `getTimeS()` at the moment of execution, storing the FPS of the application for future reference.
items.name: init

items.description: calculates the time difference between two points and returns it as a float value. It takes advantage of the `getTimeS()` method to retrieve the current time and compares it with the previous frame time, stored in the `lastFrame` field.

returns.description: a floating-point value representing the time difference between two frames.

items.name: calculateDelta

items.description: updates the frame rate by incrementing a counter every second, storing the current frame rate and resetting it after 10 seconds have passed since the last update.
items.name: updateFPS

items.description: returns the `delta` variable, which is a `float` value that contains the difference between two values.

returns.description: a floating-point value representing the delta.

items.name: getDelta

items.description: retrieves the current frame rate per second (FPS) and returns it as a floating-point number.

returns.description: the current frame rate of the application in floats.

items.name: getFPS

items.description: retrieves the value of a `time` field, returning it as a floating-point number.

returns.description: a floating-point representation of the current time.

items.name: getTime

items.description: updates FPS, calculates and stores delta time, and increments time.
items.name: update

structured.description: A `Transform` class that represents a transformation in 3D space. It has various methods for rotating, looking at a point, and setting transformations. The code also provides functionality for tracking changes to the transformation and applying them to the transformation matrix. Additionally, it includes a parent-child relationship between transformations, where the child transformation can inherit its transformations from its parent.

items.description: is a representation of an object's position, rotation, and scale in a 3D space. It has various methods for updating these values, such as `update()`, `rotate()`, and `setParent()`. The class also provides a transformation matrix that can be used to transform the object in the 3D space. Additionally, it has methods for getting and setting the position, rotation, and scale of the object.
items.name: Transform

items.description: updates the reference values of `pos`, `rot`, and `scale` based on the current values of the same properties. If any of the reference values have changed, the corresponding field is set to the new value.

usage.code:
Transform transform = new Transform(); // Create a new transform object
transform.setPos(new Vector3f(1, 2, 3)); // Set the position of the transform to (1, 2, 3)
transform.update(); // Update the transformation matrix of the transform

items.name: update

items.description: rotates a `Quaternion` instance by an angle around a specified axis, resulting in a new `Quaternion` instance that represents the rotated orientation.

params.name: axis
params.description:
3D vector that defines the axis of rotation.

* `axis` is a `Vector3f`, representing a 3D vector with floating-point values for its x, y, and z components.
* The `x`, `y`, and `z` attributes of `axis` represent the coordinates of the axis along the x, y, and z axes, respectively.

params.name: angle
params.description: 3D rotation angle of the axis around which the rotation will occur.

usage.description: This example creates a new Transform object and then rotates it by the given vector (in this case, the x-axis) and angle. The rotation is applied to the current rotation of the transform.

items.name: rotate

items.description: computes the rotation required to face a given point while maintaining a specific up direction. The rotation is returned as a quaternion object.

params.name: point
params.description:
3D position that the camera should look at.

* `point` is a `Vector3f` object representing a 3D point in space.
* `up` is a `Vector3f` object representing a direction in space, which is used to calculate the rotation needed to look at the specified point from the camera's current position.

params.name: up
params.description:
3D direction along which the entity will look at the point passed as argument.

* The `Vector3f` class represents a 3D vector in homogeneous coordinates, consisting of x, y, and z components.
* The `getLookAtRotation()` method calculates the rotation necessary to look at a given point from a specific viewpoint, as represented by the `up` parameter.
* The resulting rotation is stored in the `rot` field for later use in rendering or other applications.

usage.code:
Transform transform = new Transform();
transform.lookAt(new Vector3f(5, 0, 0), Vector3f(0, 1, 0)); // looks at the point (5, 0, 0) with the up vector (0, 1, 0).

items.name: lookAt

items.description: computes a quaternion representation of the rotation from the camera's current position to look at a specified point, using the `pos` and `up` parameters as reference frames.

params.name: point
params.description:
3D position that the rotation is to be applied around, which is used to calculate the rotation matrix.

* `point`: A 3D vector representing the point to look at. It has three components (x, y, z) representing the position of the point in the 3D space.
* `up`: A 3D vector representing the direction of the up axis. Its components are (0, 0, 1), indicating that it points directly upwards in the 3D space.

params.name: up
params.description:
3D vector that defines the "up" direction in the rotation, which is used to calculate the quaternion representing the look-at rotation.

* `up`: A `Vector3f` object representing a vector that is perpendicular to both the forward direction and the right-hand rule. This property allows for the rotation to be calculated based on the cross product of this vector with the difference between the `point` and the current position.

returns.description:
a quaternion representing the rotation needed to look at a point from a specific position and direction.

* The output is a `Quaternion` object that represents the rotation required to look at a point in 3D space from a specific position and direction.
* The quaternion is generated using the rotation matrix method, where the input vector is subtracted from the position vector, normalized, and then multiplied by the up vector to obtain the rotation axis.
* The resulting quaternion represents the rotation around the rotation axis, with the point as the center of rotation.
* The quaternion can be used to rotate a 3D object or camera to look at the specified point from the given position and direction.

items.name: getLookAtRotation

items.description: evaluates whether an object has changed by checking its parent, position, rotation, and scale, returning `true` if any have changed and `false` otherwise.

returns.description: a boolean value indicating whether the object has changed in any of its properties.

usage.description: The example creates a new Transform object and assigns values to its position, rotation, and scale. It then calls the hasChanged() method on the transform object to check if any of its properties have been modified since the last update(). The return value is stored in the changed variable and printed to the console. In this case, because all properties of the Transform are set to default values when created, the returned boolean will be false.

items.name: hasChanged

items.description: calculates a transformation matrix based on an object's position, rotation, and scale, and returns it as a Matrix4f object.

returns.description:
a transformed matrix representing the combination of translation, rotation, and scaling.

* The matrix is a product of three matrices: `getParentMatrix()`, `translationMatrix`, and `rotationMatrix`. Each of these matrices represents a transformation in 3D space.
* `getParentMatrix()` refers to the transformation applied to the object's parent, which is used to calculate the final transformation.
* `translationMatrix` represents a translation in 3D space, with three elements (x, y, and z) that specify the offset from the origin.
* `rotationMatrix` represents a rotation of the object around its center, with nine elements (r, g, b, x, y, and z) that specify the amount of rotation in different directions.
* `scaleMatrix` represents a scaling of the object, with three elements (x, y, and z) that specify the new size of the object after scaling.

The resulting matrix is a composition of these transformations, which can be used to apply the desired transformation to an object in 3D space.

usage.description: This code creates an instance of the Transform class and sets its position, rotation, and scale using the setPos, setRot, and setScale methods. It then calls the getTransformation method on the Transform instance to retrieve a Matrix4f object that represents the transformation of the object. The resulting transformation matrix can be used to transform vertices or other data in the 3D world.

items.name: getTransformation

items.description: retrieves and returns the transformation matrix of its parent node in a hierarchical tree structure, taking into account changes to the parent node's transformation.

returns.description:
a Matrix4f object representing the parent transformation matrix.

* `parentMatrix`: A `Matrix4f` object representing the parent transformation matrix of the current transform node in the hierarchy.
* `hasChanged()`: A boolean method that indicates whether the parent transformation matrix has changed since the last time it was accessed. If `hasChanged()` is `true`, then the `getTransformation()` method of the `parent` object returns a new transformation matrix.

items.name: getParentMatrix

items.description: sets the `parent` field of the current object to a given `Transform` instance, allowing for the inheritance of properties and methods from the parent object.

params.name: parent
params.description:
3D transform to which the current transform will be attached as a child, effectively creating a hierarchical relationship between the two transforms.

* The `Transform` class is used as the type of the `parent` parameter, indicating that it is an object of this class.
* The `this.parent = parent;` statement assigns the value of the `parent` parameter to the `parent` field of the current instance.

In summary, the `setParent` function modifies the `parent` field of the current instance with the value provided in the input parameter.

items.name: setParent

items.description: transforms a position vector using the matrix provided by the parent component, and returns the transformed position.

returns.description:
a transformed position vector.

The output is a `Vector3f` object that represents the transformed position of the GameObject in question, based on the transformation applied by the parent matrix. The vector contains the x, y, and z components of the transformed position, which can be used for various purposes such as rendering, collision detection, or other game logic operations.

items.name: getTransformedPos

items.description: takes a `Quaternion` object `parentRotation` and multiplies it by another `Quaternion` object `rot`, returning the transformed rotation.

returns.description:
a Quaternion representing the transformed rotation of the parent object based on its own rotation and the given rotation.

* `Quaternion parentRotation`: This is the rotation of the parent node in the tree, represented as a Quaternion object.
* `rot`: This is the rotation of the current node in the tree, also represented as a Quaternion object. The multiplication between `parentRotation` and `rot` is performed to generate the transformed rotation of the current node.

items.name: getTransformedRot

items.description: returns the position of an object in a three-dimensional space, represented as a vector in the form (x, y, z).

returns.description:
a `Vector3f` object representing the position of the entity.

* `pos`: A Vector3f object that represents the position of the game object in 3D space, with x, y, and z components.
* The values of these components can range from -10 to 10 for each dimension, indicating the possible positions of the object within the game environment.

usage.description: In this example, a new instance of the Transform class is created and set to have a position of (1, 2, 3). The getPos method is then called on this instance, which returns the current position of the transform. The output of the program will be the vector (1, 2, 3), which matches the position that was previously set.

items.name: getPos

items.description: sets the position of an object to a new value provided as a parameter.

params.name: pos
params.description:
3D position of an object, which is assigned to the `pos` field of the `Object` class in the function call.

* `this.pos`: It is a field of type Vector3f in the class where the function is defined. This field represents the position of an object in 3D space.

usage.code:
Vector3f newPosition = new Vector3f(5.0, 10.0, 2.0);
transform.setPos(newPosition);
usage.description: This will change the position of transform to (5.0, 10.0, 2.0).

items.name: setPos

items.description: adds a vector to the position component of its caller, updating the position field accordingly.

params.name: addVec
params.description:
3D vector that adds to the current position of the object.

* ` Vector3f`: This class represents a 3D vector in homogeneous coordinates, consisting of three components: x, y, and z. It has getters and setters for each component, as well as methods for adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing the vector by a scalar value.

items.name: addToPos

items.description: returns a `Quaternion` object representing the rotation of an entity.

returns.description:
a Quaternion object representing the rotation of the game object.

* `rot`: This is a Quaternion object representing the rotation of the gameObject in question. It contains x, y, z components representing the real and imaginary parts of the quaternion.

items.name: getRot

items.description: sets the rotational orientation of an object by assigning a Quaternion value to a member variable 'rot'.

params.name: rotation
params.description:
4-dimensional quaternion value that updates the rotational orientation of the object being referenced by the `this` keyword.

* The `Quaternion` class is used to represent the rotation.
* It has four attributes: `x`, `y`, `z`, and `w`, which correspond to the real and imaginary parts of the quaternion.
* The `x`, `y`, and `z` values represent the axis of rotation, while `w` represents the magnitude or length of the quaternion.

items.name: setRot

items.description: retrieves the current scale value of an object, which is a `Vector3f` instance variable named `scale`.

returns.description:
a `Vector3f` object containing the scale value.

The `Vector3f` object `scale` represents a 3D vector with three components: x, y, and z. These components represent the scale factors for each dimension of the vector, which can be used to transform or manipulate the vector in various ways.

items.name: getScale

items.description: sets the scale of an object, which is represented by a `Vector3f` instance, to a new value.

params.name: scale
params.description:
3D scaling factor for the game object, which is applied to its position, size, and orientation.

* `Vector3f`: This indicates that the input scale is a 3D vector containing three floating-point numbers representing the X, Y, and Z components of the scale.

usage.description: This code creates a new Transform object and sets its scale to (2, 2, 2) using the setScale method.

items.name: setScale

items.description: returns an empty string, indicating that it does not provide any information about its input or output.

returns.description:
an empty string.

The returned string is an empty string, indicating that the object does not have any inherent value that can be represented in a string.

The lack of a non-empty string returned by the `toString` function highlights the object's fundamental property of having no meaningful representation as an immutable object. This property makes it unique among objects and is crucial for understanding its behavior when interacting with other objects or systems.

items.name: toString

structured.description: Various utility classes and methods for manipulating data buffers, matrices, and strings. The Util class provides methods for creating and manipulating floating-point and integer buffers using BufferUtils, as well as methods for converting lists of integers to integers, floats, or strings. Additionally, the code includes a method for creating a flipped buffer and another for converting a matrix into a list of doubles.

items.description: provides various methods for manipulating and transforming arrays of data types such as integers, floats, and bytes. These methods include creating new buffers, flipping buffers, and converting between different data types. Additionally, the class provides a method for removing empty strings from an array.
items.name: Util

items.description: creates a new `FloatBuffer` object with the specified size. The buffer is created using the `BufferUtils` class, which provides utility methods for working with buffers.

params.name: size
params.description: number of elements to be stored in the resulting `FloatBuffer`.

returns.description:
a `FloatBuffer` object with the specified size.

* The function returns a `FloatBuffer` object, which is a type-safe and efficient way to manipulate floating-point data in Java.
* The `size` parameter passed to the function determines the number of elements in the buffer.
* The buffer is created using the `BufferUtils` class, which provides utility methods for working with buffers in Java.

items.name: createFloatBuffer

items.description: creates an `IntBuffer` instance with the specified size, using the `BufferUtils` class to handle the buffer creation.

params.name: size
params.description: capacity of the IntBuffer that will be created.

returns.description:
an `IntBuffer` object representing a contiguous block of integers of the specified size.

* The function returns an `IntBuffer` object, which is a type-safe wrapper around a native `int[]` array.
* The size parameter determines the capacity of the buffer, and it must be non-negative.
* The buffer is created using the `BufferUtils` class, which provides a set of utility methods for working with buffers in a safe and efficient manner.

items.name: createIntBuffer

items.description: creates a new ByteBuffer instance with a given size. The returned buffer can be used for reading and writing binary data.

params.name: size
params.description: maximum capacity of the byte buffer to be created, which determines the number of bytes that can be stored in the buffer.

returns.description:
a non-null, non-empty ByteBuffer object with the specified size.

* The input parameter `size` represents the capacity of the buffer, which is used to create a new instance of the `ByteBuffer` class.
* The `ByteBuffer` instance returned by the function is immutable and thread-safe, making it suitable for use in various applications.
* The buffer can be accessed and manipulated using various methods provided by the `ByteBuffer` class, such as `get`, `put', `mark', and `clear'.
* The `createByteBuffer' function does not modify the input parameter, ensuring that the original size remains unchanged after the call.

items.name: createByteBuffer

items.description: creates an `IntBuffer` object from a list of integers and flips it to create a view of the buffer with the elements in reverse order.

returns.description:
an `IntBuffer` object that contains the input values in a flipped state.

1. The output is an `IntBuffer`, which means it is a view of a portion of the underlying memory as an array of integers.
2. The buffer is created by copying the input array `values` into a new `IntBuffer`.
3. The buffer is flipped using the `flip()` method, which makes all the elements of the buffer inaccessible for writing and accessible for reading.

Therefore, the output of the `createFlippedBuffer` function is an `IntBuffer` that contains a copy of the input array `values`, with the added property of being flipped for read-only access.

items.name: createFlippedBuffer

items.description: creates a new FloatBuffer by copying a provided array of floating-point values into it, then flipping the buffer to enable random access to its elements.

returns.description:
a flipped `FloatBuffer` containing the input values.

* The `FloatBuffer` object returned is created by calling the `createFloatBuffer` method with the length of the input array as an argument.
* The `buffer.put(values)` method is called to store the input array values in the buffer.
* The `buffer.flip()` method is called to flip the buffer, making its data available for reading from the beginning of the buffer.

The output of the `createFlippedBuffer` function is a `FloatBuffer` object that contains the input array values in a reversed order, starting from the end of the buffer and moving towards the beginning.

items.name: createFlippedBuffer

items.description: removes empty strings from an array of strings, returning an array of non-empty strings.

params.name: data
params.description:
0-length array of strings to be filtered, which is then processed by the function to produce an output array of non-empty string elements.

* `data` is an array of strings.
* Its length is defined as `data.length`.
* Each element in the array is a string object.
* The elements may be empty strings, represented by an empty string value.

returns.description: an array of non-empty strings.

items.name: removeEmptyStrings

items.description: converts an array of integers into an integer array with the same length. It does so by creating a new integer array and copying each element from the original array to the new one.

params.name: data
params.description:
Integer array that contains the values to be converted into an integer array.

* It is an instance of `Integer[]`.
* Its length is specified by the function parameter `length`.
* Each element in the array is a distinct integer value.

returns.description: an integer array with the same length as the input `data` array, containing the corresponding integers from the input array.

items.name: toIntArray

items.description: converts a `List<Integer>` to an `int[]` array, copying the list's elements into the array.

params.name: data
params.description:
List of integers that are converted to an integer array by the `toIntArray()` method.

* `data`: A list of integers that is passed as input to the function.
* `size()`: This method returns the number of elements in the list, which is used to create an array of the same size.
* `get(int index)`: This method retrieves the value of a specific element in the list at the given index. The method throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` if the index is out of range (i.e., negative or greater than the list's size).

returns.description: an integer array with the same size as the input list of integers.

items.name: toIntArray

items.description: converts a `Float` array to a `float` array, copying the values of the original array to the new one.

params.name: data
params.description:
1D array of `Float` values that are to be converted into a new 1D array of `float` values.

* `data`: The input parameter is an array of `Float`.
* Length: The length of the `data` array matches the length of the returned `result` array.
* Data elements: Each element in the `data` array is assigned to a corresponding position in the `result` array.

returns.description: an array of floating-point values, containing the same values as the input `data` array.

items.name: toFloatArray

items.description: takes a list of floating-point numbers and returns an array of the same size containing the corresponding float values.

params.name: data
params.description:
list of floating-point numbers that will be converted into an array of identical floats.

* The type of `data` is `List<Float>`, which means it's an array of floating-point numbers.
* The size of `data` is equal to its number of elements, which can be accessed through the `size()` method.
* Each element in `data` is a `Float` value, which is why the function uses a `for` loop to iterate over the elements and copy them into the resulting array.

returns.description: a floating-point array containing the values of the given list of floats.

items.name: toFloatArray

structured.description: A `Quaternion` class that represents 4D rotational transformations in 3D space. It provides various methods for converting between rotational matrices and quaternions, as well as methods for computing angles and distances related to rotations. The code also includes utility functions for working with rotations, such as rotation interpolation and extrapolation, and conversion between different coordinate systems.
